name: Cheese and Herb Flat Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Soften yeast in 1/4 cup warm water.
2. In a mixing bowl, combine 2 tablespoons margarine, sugar, and salt with scalded milk. Cool to lukewarm.
3. Stir the yeast into the milk mixture. Gradually add flour to form a stiff dough. You may not need all of the flour. Knead on a floured surface until smooth and satiny; about 4 to 5 minutes. Place in a greased bowl and turn to coat the top. Cover and let rise until light, about 45 minutes.
4. Divide the dough into two halves. Press each piece into a 9-inch pie or cake pan.
5. Combine chopped onions, melted margarine, oregano, paprika, celery seed, garlic salt, basil. Spread over the dough. Sprinkle evenly with cheese. Prick in several places with a fork.
6. Let rise for about 30 minutes or until light.
7. Bake in a preheated oven at 375 degrees for 20 to 25 minutes until golden brown. Serve while still warm.
Yield: 2 9-inch flat breads.
